page.title: Validation
page.lead: Discover SkyguardJS's powerful validation system. It provides built-in request validation without installing dependencies, ensuring data integrity with less overhead.

basic.title: Basic validation
basic.description: To create validation schemas, use the <mark class="docs-highlight">v</mark> builder to define rules, the <mark class="docs-highlight">schema</mark> function to create the schema, and the <mark class="docs-highlight">validationRequest()</mark> middleware to register it on a specific route.

types.title: Type validation
types.description: Skyguard supports multiple data types with built-in validation:

optionalFields.title: Optional fields
optionalFields.description: You can define optional fields or default values, since fields are required by default:

converter.title: Type converter
converter.description: Skyguard provides a type converter that transforms input into the data type you specify:
converter.tip: You can use the type converter to validate params or query values in requests.

differentParts.title: Validating different parts
differentParts.description: Skyguard lets you validate different parts of the request:

nestedObjects.title: Nested objects
nestedObjects.description: With <mark class="docs-highlight">v.object()</mark> validation, you can validate complex nested structures:

arrayValidation.title: Array validation
arrayValidation.description: You can validate arrays that contain objects:

errorHandling.title: Error handling
errorHandling.description: When validation fails, Skyguard automatically returns a 400 error with details:
errorHandling.warn: Validation errors are handled automatically. You do not need to write custom error-handling code.
